prompt: You are reviewing the R package in the current working directory for an rOpenSci software-review issue. Analyse its dependencies and produce a report I can attach to the review issue. Tell me which dependencies are genuinely worth removing, and back every recommendation with evidence.
assertions: Builds the recursive dependency tree with node/edge counts
Separates prunable leaves from packages pinned by core deps
Argues removal only counts if it prunes the install tree
Counts real usage (call sites) of imported functions
Assesses base-R feasibility of replacing a dependency
Reproduces the triggering prompt in the report
Names the skill/method that produced the report
States no package files were modified (read-only)
Keeps deps whose removal buys nothing and says why
